title: jatropha hildebrandtii
intro: Jatropha hildebrandtii, also known as the Devil's Back Bone, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and aiding in the management of rheumatism. In traditional medicine, Jatropha hildebrandtii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used topically in the form of a poultice or ointment.
